name: BS Abriko
classification_notes: A single-style display typeface built on Bauhaus geometric principles with deliberate irregularities, expressive counter forms and unconventional proportions; firmly in the display category with no text weight variants.
summary: A single-style geometric display face by Maël Bächtold, drawing on Bauhaus letter forms with deliberate irregularities and playful counter shapes.
description: Designed by Maël Bächtold at Bolid System and released in 2025, it pairs Bauhaus-era geometric structure with intentional naivety: roundness is pushed, counter forms are tested, and some elements are omitted from glyphs where convention might demand them. Despite those irregularities, the contrasts are carefully weighted to hold together as a coherent whole. Available as a single style, it suits editorial headlines, packaging and branding where an unconventional yet warm character is wanted.
foundry_blurb: Bolid System describe BS Abriko as a free-minded, impulsive display typeface born from Maël Bächtold's fascination with geometric drawing and the expressive display fonts of the Bauhaus era. They note that it experiments with roundness, counter forms and unexpected proportions, and that despite its playful irregularities every detail is deliberate, with contrasts balanced to maintain visual unity even where elements are placed unconventionally or omitted entirely.

name: BS Acapulko
classification_notes: A formal copperplate script drawing from 18th-century pointed-nib calligraphic traditions, specifically referencing Charles Snell's work, with a moderate slant and calligraphy-inspired letterforms refined for digital use.
summary: A digitally refined copperplate script in two styles, drawing on 18th-century calligraphic tradition and specifically the work of Charles Snell.
description: Designed by Arthur Schwarz and released in 2025, it takes the formal conventions of copperplate script and reworks them for screen and print with a moderate slant, soft curves and distinctive details at terminals and ligatures. The family offers two styles, Script and Italic, with notable glyph features including sf and st ligatures and unconnected strokes on a, g, d and q. Broad Latin language support covers several hundred languages across extended Latin ranges.
foundry_blurb: Bolid System describe BS Acapulko as a copperplate script that captures the elegance and formality of the 18th century while applying modern digital type design techniques. Inspired by Charles Snell's approach to simple and effective form, it departs from traditional pointed-nib drawing in favour of a more versatile, digitally refined result. Available in Script and Italic styles, it features a moderate slant, smooth curves and a range of strong typographic details including sf and st ligatures and unconnected letterforms.

name: BS Milkshake Display
classification_notes: A display typeface with voluptuous curves, swash alternates and generous ink traps that evoke 1970s lettering; its mix of soft drops and sharp edges places it firmly in the expressive display category.
summary: A 10-weight display serif inspired by 1970s lettering, built around voluptuous curves, sharp edges and an extensive set of swash alternates.
description: Designed by Maël Bächtold at Bolid System and released in 2025, it channels anemoia through forms that balance soft, generous drops against precise angular details. Ten upright weights run from Thin to Black, backed by a variable font, with swash alternates on most letters adding movement and personality without sacrificing legibility. Thin, low-contrast brackets and parentheses reinforce its identity as a typeface made for large, expressive settings.
foundry_blurb: Bolid System describe BS Milkshake Display as a typeface born from anemoia, a longing for a time never experienced, drawing on the spirit of the 1970s. Its forms balance voluptuous curves and generous drops against sharp, precise edges, with swash alternates extending the expressive flow. Available in ten upright weights plus a variable font, it is positioned as a versatile tool that blends funkiness, friendliness and precision.

name: BS Notion
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a geometric typeface rooted in early grotesque tradition, with clean straight terminals and slightly condensed proportions characteristic of geometric sans design.
summary: A ten-weight geometric sans from Swiss foundry Bolid System, drawing on early grotesque traditions with straight terminals, slightly condensed proportions and optional circular alternates.
description: Designed by Maël Bächtold and released in 2025, BS Notion spans Hairline to Black with matching italics across 20 styles, plus a variable font. Straight terminals on characters like a, f, g, r, t and Q give it a functional, utilitarian character, while two sets of OpenType alternates let the designer shift tone: one converts oval terminals to near-circles, the other rounds punctuation and diacritics for a softer texture. Its slightly condensed proportions make it efficient on the page without sacrificing typographic rhythm.
foundry_blurb: Bolid System describe BS Notion as a geometric typeface with a pragmatic spirit and understated elegance, rooted in early grotesque-inspired fonts. They note its slightly condensed proportions suit it particularly well as a text typeface, and highlight two flexible alternate sets that allow the designer to move from a utilitarian to a subtly expressive tone.

name: BS Resort Sans
classification_notes: A low-contrast, slightly condensed grotesque sans designed for UI and extended text, with rigorous structure and a warm, accessible character through OpenType alternates.
summary: A low-contrast grotesque by Arthur Schwarz designed for UI and extended text, spanning 10 weights with both 13° and 26° italic variants.
description: Sharing its structural DNA with the BS Resort family, Resort Sans strips away contrast for a more economical, screen-friendly form. Slightly condensed proportions help conserve space without sacrificing reading comfort, and OpenType alternates allow the tone to shift from friendly to formal by swapping dots, punctuation and letter forms. The 30-style family includes a variable font covering both weight and slant axes.
foundry_blurb: Bolid System describe Resort Sans as a 'normhardcore' sans-serif family optimised for maximum legibility, reinterpreting the architecture of the BS Resort family with a low-contrast approach suited to UI design, interfaces and extended text. The family spans 10 weights with both Italic (13°) and Super Italic (26°) companions, totalling 30 styles plus a variable font, and includes extensive OpenType alternates that shift the aesthetic from warm and friendly to strict and formal.

name: BS Resort Classic
classification_notes: A deliberate hybrid: neo-grotesque structure with Didone-level vertical contrast, making it neither a pure sans nor a serif revival but a display-oriented invention that sits closest to the high-contrast sans subcategory.
summary: A sans serif with Didone-level vertical contrast designed by Arthur Schwarz, spanning ten weights each with a 13° italic and a 26° super italic.
description: Rather than reviving or referencing a historical model, Arthur Schwarz built BS Resort Classic as an artificial invention: a neo-grotesque skeleton carrying the dramatic stroke contrast normally reserved for Didone serifs. Details such as the forward-leaning S and the exaggerated upper stroke of the f introduce deliberate disruptions to grotesque convention. The 30-style family includes a full set of alternates for a, g, l and t, rounded punctuation variants, and a two-axis variable font covering weight and slant.
foundry_blurb: Bolid System describe BS Resort Classic as a typographic hybrid display typeface whose structure follows the clarity of a neo-grotesque while its strong vertical contrast draws from the Didone tradition. They are explicit that it is neither a revival nor a homage but an artificial invention, exploring what contrast in sans serif design can become when detached from its historical context. The family spans ten weights from Thin to Black, each with a standard italic at 13° and a super italic at 26°, totalling 30 styles plus a variable font.

name: BS Staahl
classification_notes: A modular, semi-geometric display typeface designed for signage and large-scale architectural use, with a constructed, brutalist character and optical size axis placing it firmly in the display category.
summary: A semi-modular display typeface born from a school signage commission in western Switzerland, built around shared outer corner radii and sharp inner junctions.
description: Designed in collaboration with Enen Studio, BS Staahl follows a semi-modular construction logic drawn from the architecture of the school it was originally commissioned for. An optical size axis runs from Elementary through Middle to High, with contrast growing more pronounced at larger sizes, while stems remain assertive throughout and diagonals stay comparatively restrained. The family ships as a variable font and includes Regular, Italic and Backslanted cuts, performing at its best set large where its unicase quality and tight spacing create visual tension.
foundry_blurb: Bolid System describe BS Staahl as a modular display typeface developed with graphic design studio Enen, originally created for a school signage system in western Switzerland. They note its semi-modular logic, whereby all outer corners share the same radius echoing the school's architecture while inner junctions remain sharply defined. The optical size axis moves from minimal to pronounced contrast across three predefined styles, and select glyphs such as M, N and W introduce opportunistic contrast to add nuance to the otherwise brutalist system.

name: BS Synthetik
classification_notes: A geometric humanist sans with very low contrast, smooth curve connections and vertical-cut terminals, positioning it between Futura and Johnston as a geometric sans with humanist warmth.
summary: A geometric sans with humanist inflections, drawing on Futura and Johnston while adding vertical-cut terminals and a set of structural alternates across ten weights.
description: Designed by Maël Bächtold and released in 2025, BS Synthetik sits between classic geometric models and contemporary practice, with very low contrast, smooth curve connections and vertical-cut terminals that give it both speed and legibility. Distinctive letterforms include an asymmetric crossbar on f and t that extends only to the right, a Q whose tail is vertically trimmed to reach beyond the bowl, and a 4 with an open triangular counterform. The family spans ten weights with matching italics and a variable font covering weight and slant axes.
foundry_blurb: Bolid System describe BS Synthetik as an expressive, minimal geometric sans-serif with generous proportions, positioned as a contemporary interpretation between Futura and Johnston. They note its geometric humanist character, very low contrast, smooth curve connections and vertical-cut terminals, along with structural details such as asymmetric crossbars on f and t, a Q with a vertically trimmed tail and an open-counterform 4. Alternates including a straight-stemmed j and a geometric-cross t allow subtle variation without disrupting the overall rhythm. The family comprises twenty styles plus a variable font.

name: BS Resort Serif
classification_notes: High-contrast vertical stress with hairline serifs on a neo-grotesque skeleton places it squarely in the Didone tradition, though its constructed, geometric underpinning keeps it from feeling purely classical.
summary: A high-contrast serif by Arthur Schwarz that grafts hairline serifs onto a neo-grotesque skeleton, spanning ten weights with both a standard italic and an extreme 26-degree super italic.
description: Built on the same structural foundation as BS Resort Classic, the addition of hairline serifs pulls the design toward a sharper, more formal register without abandoning its constructed, methodical character. Thirty static styles run from Thin to Black, each offered in upright, italic at 13 degrees, and super italic at 26 degrees, with a two-axis variable font covering weight and slant. Alternate single-storey forms for the a and g, a curved-terminal l, and a straight-terminal t give designers granular control over the typeface's personality across different contexts.
foundry_blurb: Bolid System describe BS Resort Serif as a high-contrast serif grounded in a neo-grotesque structure, sharing its foundation with BS Resort Classic but shifted toward something sharper and more formal through the introduction of hairline serifs. The construction, they note, remains strict and proportioned, methodical rather than ornamental, embracing the Didone tradition while retaining a constructed, contemporary edge. The S retains a distinctive forward lean, the f features a striking upper curve, and the family is available with customisation and language extensions.
